Validating whether the logical steps from reported numbers to broad conclusions are sound is entailed by the delegation, making this dimension active though secondary to R1.3.","R1.3":"The delegation's load-bearing responsibility. The artifact makes three verifiable evidence-claim pairs: (i) 'better-calibrated routing across all task families' — the KL evidence contradicts this (0.92 > 0.47 is worse, not better); (ii) 'favorable KL value for StanRoute' — directly falsified by the reported numbers; (iii) 'code-generation gain establishes uniform improvement' — the reported per-family evidence is insufficient to support a universal claim over six families. rationale.R1.2: The artifact infers 'uniform improvement across all task families' from a single family (code generation) without presenting per-family data for remaining five families; separately, it interprets StanRoute's KL=0.92 as 'favorable' relative to baseline KL=0.47 — a directional error (lower KL is better). Validating whether the logical steps from reported numbers to broad conclusions are sound is entailed by the delegation, making this dimension active though secondary to R1.3.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation's load-bearing responsibility. The artifact makes three verifiable evidence-claim pairs: (i) 'better-calibrated routing across all task families' — the KL evidence contradicts this (0.92 > 0.47 is worse, not better); (ii) 'favorable KL value for StanRoute' — directly falsified by the reported numbers; (iii) 'code-generation gain establishes uniform improvement' — the reported per-family evidence is insufficient to support a universal claim over six families. All three require explicit evidence-to-claim audit.
